name: risk-flagging
description: Proactively identify project risks from data signals, categorize by severity, assess impact probability, and recommend mitigations
objectives: Scan project data for risk signals (delays, blockers, scope changes, resource issues)
Categorize risks by type (schedule, resource, technical, external, scope)
Assess probability and impact for each risk
Recommend mitigation strategies with effort estimates
Maintain a living risk register updated with each assessment
prerequisites: Active project with tasks, assignments, and timeline data
Historical project data for pattern recognition

procedure.description: Scan project data for risk indicators
commands: CHECK tasks overdue or approaching deadline with low completion
DETECT velocity decline trends (last 3 data points)
IDENTIFY blocked tasks with no resolution path
SCAN for scope additions without timeline adjustments
CHECK team over-allocation or single points of failure
DETECT external dependency delays
MONITOR communication gaps (no updates in >3 days)
procedure.expectedOutput: List of detected risk signals with data evidence

procedure.description: Categorize and assess each identified risk
commands: CLASSIFY risk type: schedule, resource, technical, external, scope, quality
ASSESS probability: high (>70%), medium (30-70%), low (<30%)
ASSESS impact: critical (project failure), major (milestone miss), moderate (delay), minor (inconvenience)
CALCULATE risk score: probability x impact
RANK risks by score for prioritization
procedure.expectedOutput: Classified risk register with probability/impact ratings

procedure.description: Determine what happens if each risk materializes
commands: MAP risk to affected tasks, milestones, and deliverables
CALCULATE timeline impact if risk materializes
IDENTIFY cascade effects through dependency chains
ASSESS cost impact (additional resources, delays, penalties)
DETERMINE affected stakeholders
procedure.expectedOutput: Impact assessment per risk with affected areas

procedure.description: Propose mitigation strategies for top risks
commands: GENERATE mitigation options for each high/critical risk
ESTIMATE effort and cost per mitigation option
IDENTIFY risk owner (person responsible for monitoring)
SET trigger conditions for mitigation activation
DEFINE contingency plans if mitigation fails
RECOMMEND preventive actions for medium risks
procedure.expectedOutput: Mitigation plan with options, owners, and triggers

procedure.description: Generate risk report and trigger appropriate alerts
commands: COMPILE risk register with all assessments
GENERATE summary for stakeholder briefing
TRIGGER alerts for critical and high risks
UPDATE risk trends (new, increasing, stable, decreasing, resolved)
SCHEDULE next risk review checkpoint
procedure.expectedOutput: Risk report with alerts and trend tracking

criteria: Risk classifications are accurate and consistent

testScenarios.expected: High schedule risk flagged with timeline impact and mitigation options
Resource risk flagged with redistribution recommendations

successIndicators: Risks identified before they become issues
No surprise project failures from undetected risks

commonErrors.error: Insufficient data for reliable risk assessment
commonErrors.solution: Flag as potential risk with low confidence, request more data

commonErrors.error: Too many low-severity risks creating noise
commonErrors.solution: Apply threshold filter, only surface medium+ risks in reports

rollbackProcedure: SUPPRESS alerts if assessment was incorrect
escalationCriteria: Critical risk with no viable mitigation

successMetrics.name: Risk Detection Rate
successMetrics.measurement: Risks identified proactively vs discovered as incidents

successMetrics.name: False Positive Rate
successMetrics.measurement: Flagged risks that never materialized and were non-issues

successMetrics.name: Mitigation Effectiveness
successMetrics.measurement: Mitigated risks that were successfully prevented or reduced

agentInterfaces.message: Fetch project health data for risk assessment
Send risk alerts to project stakeholders
